title: subworkflow - aws stop ec2 instance
description:
Subworkflow to stop an EC2 instance using AWS Systems Manager Automation

Parameters:

instanceId: (Required) The EC2 instance ID to stop
awsregion: (Required) The AWS region
force: (Optional) Force stop without graceful shutdown (true/false)
dynatraceawsconnection: (Required) Dynatrace AWS OIDC connection
guide:
## ⏹️ **Subworkflow to Stop an EC2 Instance**

🛑 This subworkflow provides an **easy way** to **stop a running EC2 instance**—without requiring any knowledge of how AWS Systems Manager (SSM) works.

It executes the stop command via the **AWS-StopEC2Instance** SSM automation document, **waits for the stop to complete**, and **validates whether the stop was successful**.  
❌ If the stop fails, the subworkflow fails to enable **restart-on-error** in the parent workflow.

---

### ✅ **Requirements**

⚠️ For this subworkflow to run successfully:
- The EC2 instance **must be in the running state**
- The EC2 instance **must be managed by AWS Systems Manager (SSM)**

📖 AWS documentation:  
https://docs.aws.amazon.com/systems-manager/latest/userguide/systems-manager-setting-up-ec2.html

---

### ⚙️ **Parameters**

- **instanceId** (Required): 🆔 The EC2 instance ID to stop.  
- **awsregion** (Required): 🌍 The AWS region where the instance resides.  
- **force** (Optional): 🛑 Force stop without graceful shutdown (true/false). Default: false.  
- **dynatraceawsconnection** (Required): 🔑 The Dynatrace AWS OIDC connection.

---

### 📘 **AWS SSM Document Used**

**Document Name**: `AWS-StopEC2Instance`

🔗 AWS Documentation:  
https://docs.aws.amazon.com/systems-manager-automation-runbooks/latest/userguide/automation-aws-stopec2instance.html

**What this runbook does:**
- Stops one or more running EC2 instances
- Optionally forces the stop
- Waits for instance to reach stopped state
- Stops billing for the instance while stopped

---

### 🧠 **Workflow Logic**

- 📤 Sends stop request to **`AWS-StopEC2Instance`** SSM automation document
- ⏹️ The EC2 instance is stopped
- ⏳ Waits for the stop operation to complete
- ✅ Returns success if stop completes
- ❌ Fails if stop fails, enabling restart-on-error

---
